function.name: query_train_info
function.description: Query train ticket information by origin, destination, date, and related conditions. Returns matching train information including train number, departure/arrival time, station information, duration, seat class, complete route-level price, etc. Supports direct and transfer routes. Direct routes show only the first segment of the complete route, while transfer routes show each segment of the real transfer chain and do not emit duplicated pseudo-segments. The returned `price` is the complete per-person reference price for the candidate route, not a segment price. In travel planning, all train-related information must strictly come from this tool's results and must not be fabricated; station names and train names must exactly match the query results. When calling this tool, origin and destination must be city names only, not railway-station names, airport names, province names, district names, or attraction names. If the user mentions aliases such as `Xi'an North Railway Station`, convert them back to the corresponding city name first. Use directOnly=true for direct-train requests. Use sortBy=price for cheapest, sortBy=duration for shortest duration, sortBy=latest_arrival for latest-arrival return trains, and sortBy=departure_time or arrival_time for time-order requests.

origin.description: Origin city name only. Do not pass a railway-station name, airport name, province name, district name, or attraction name

destination.description: Destination city name only. Do not pass a railway-station name, airport name, province name, district name, or attraction name

seatClassName.description: Train seat class: First Class Seat, Second Class Seat,Business Seat

trainType.description: Optional train type or marketing transport name, such as G, D, C, K, T, or Z; use when the user requests the cheapest train within a specific train type

directOnly.description: Whether to return only direct trains; set to true when the user requests direct trains

sortBy.description: Result sort order; use price for cheapest, departure_time for earliest departure, arrival_time for earliest arrival, latest_arrival for latest arrival, and duration for shortest train duration

depTimeStart.description: Optional departure-time window start in HH:MM when the user specifies a departure period

depTimeEnd.description: Optional departure-time window end in HH:MM when the user specifies a departure period

arrTimeStart.description: Optional arrival-time window start in HH:MM when the user specifies an arrival period

arrTimeEnd.description: Optional arrival-time window end in HH:MM when the user specifies an arrival period

function.name: query_flight_info
function.description: Query flight information by origin, destination, date, and related conditions. Returns matching flight information including flight number, departure/arrival time, airport information, duration, seat class, complete route-level price, aircraft type, etc. Supports direct and connecting flights. Direct flights show only the first segment of the complete route, while connecting flights show each segment of the real connection chain and do not emit duplicated pseudo-segments. The returned `price` is the complete per-person reference price for the candidate route, not a segment price. In travel planning, all flight-related information must strictly come from this tool's results and must not be fabricated; airport names and flight numbers must exactly match the query results. When calling this tool, origin and destination must be city names only, not airport names, railway-station names, province names, district names, or attraction names. If the user mentions aliases such as `Shanghai City` or `Shanghai Pudong International Airport`, convert them back to the corresponding city name first.

origin.description: Origin city name only. Do not pass an airport name, railway-station name, province name, district name, or attraction name

destination.description: Destination city name only. Do not pass an airport name, railway-station name, province name, district name, or attraction name

depTimeStart.description: Optional departure-time window start in HH:MM when the user specifies a departure period

depTimeEnd.description: Optional departure-time window end in HH:MM when the user specifies a departure period

arrTimeStart.description: Optional arrival-time window start in HH:MM when the user specifies an arrival period

arrTimeEnd.description: Optional arrival-time window end in HH:MM when the user specifies an arrival period

seatClassName.description: Flight seat class: Economy Class, Business Class, First Class

directOnly.description: Whether to return only direct flights; set to true when the user requests direct flights

sortBy.description: Result sort order; use price for cheapest, departure_time for earliest departure, arrival_time for earliest arrival, latest_arrival for latest arrival or arrival-window checks, and duration for shortest flight duration

function.name: query_hotel_info
function.description: Query hotel information by destination, hotel star rating, hotel name, hotel brand, service, price range, and ranking preference. In travel planning, all hotel-related information must strictly come from the results returned by this tool and must not be fabricated; hotel names must be exactly the same as the query results. Use sortBy=price for cheapest hotel requests, sortBy=rating for highest-rated requests, and sortBy=decoration for newest-decoration requests. Use requiredService when the user requires a specific hotel service such as robot room service, gym, free parking, or SPA service.

hotelName.description: Exact hotel name. Use this when the user specifies a concrete hotel, apartment, or homestay name, e.g. Hong Kong Aegean Sea Theme Apartment

hotelBrands.description: Hotel brand, only supports single value query. Optional brands: Ji Hotel, Atour Hotel, Marriott, Hilton, Home Inn, Jinjiang Inn, Hanting Hotel, Orange Hotel

minPrice.description: Minimum nightly price; use when the user gives a hotel price range

maxPrice.description: Maximum nightly price; use when the user gives a hotel price range or accommodation budget cap

requiredService.description: Required hotel service, e.g. Robot room service, Gym, Free parking, SPA service, Washer and dryer, or TV screen casting

sortBy.description: Result sort order; use price for cheapest, rating for highest-rated, and decoration for newest-decoration hotel requests

function.name: query_attraction_details
function.description: Query attraction detailed information. Based on the attraction name, returns useful information including attraction name, city, rating, opening hours, recommended visit duration, ticket price, attraction type, and crowd/queue notes.

function.name: recommend_attractions
function.description: Search and return popular attraction information for a city based on city keyword. Each item exposes an explicit `Attraction Name` field plus description and type. During travel plan generation, all attraction information must strictly come from the results returned by this tool, and attraction names must be copied exactly from `Attraction Name` without abbreviation or renaming.

attraction_type.description: Attraction type filter (enum), options: 'Historical and Cultural', 'Natural Scenery', 'Art Exhibition', 'City Landmark', 'Theme Park', 'Leisure Experience'.

function.name: search_location
function.description: Query the latitude and longitude coordinates corresponding to a place name. For example, inputting 'Beijing South Railway Station' returns its coordinates, with values retained to six decimal places. In travel plans, all involved place names must strictly come from the results returned by other tools and must be exactly the same.

place_name.description: The place name to query, for example: 'Lao She Teahouse'

function.name: query_road_route_info
function.description: Coordinate-level fallback only. Calculate and return road/walking/taxi route details between two exact coordinates, including distance, estimated duration, and estimated cost. This tool does not resolve place names and does not query metro/subway lines or stations. For named places or any metro/subway, fewer-transfer, less-walking, budget-first, or time-first preference, use query_city_transport_plan instead. Use this tool only when exact tool-derived coordinates are already available and subway/line planning is not needed. Cost is per trip/vehicle by default.

origin.description: Origin coordinates (format: latitude,longitude), precision retained to 6 decimal places

destination.description: Destination coordinates (format: latitude,longitude), precision retained to 6 decimal places

function.name: recommend_restaurants
function.description: Recommend restaurant information near a named attraction/place by nearby_attraction_name from the restaurant database. The returned list contains restaurant name, price per person, rating, business hours, cuisine, tags, nearby place, and distance_meters when coordinates are available. Use sort_by=distance for closest/nearest requests, sort_by=price for cheap/cheapest/budget dining, and sort_by=rating for highest-rated requests.

nearby_attraction_name.description: Named nearby attraction/place from the user request or tool result. Keep the name as exact as possible, e.g., China Art Window Ltd.

sort_by.description: Optional ranking mode. Use distance for closest/nearest, price for cheap/cheapest/budget, rating for highest-rated, and auto when no comparative preference is specified.

function.name: query_restaurant_details
function.description: Query detailed information about a restaurant. Based on the restaurant name, returns restaurant name, price per person, cuisine, opening time, closing time, nearby place, rating, tags, and related details. If restaurant coordinates are needed, call search_location with the restaurant name.

function.name: query_city_transport_plan
function.description: Query an intra-city transportation recommendation between two places. The tool looks up coordinates only by exact place names or aliases explicitly registered in database location_aliases.csv, then combines direct distance, traveler preference, and the city metro graph to recommend walking, taxi, or metro. If metro is selected, it returns the nearest stations, station path, estimated walking distance, duration, and fare.

city.description: City name for the current intra-city trip, for example: Beijing

origin_place.description: Origin place name. Must exactly match tool-returned place names

destination_place.description: Destination place name. Must exactly match tool-returned place names

traveler_preference.description: Optional. Travel preference: balanced, budget_first, min_walking, time_first

function.name: query_city_weather
function.description: Query daily weather information for a city and date, including condition summary, temperature range, precipitation, and travel-planning advice. Use this tool before scheduling intercity transport, outdoor attractions, or long walking segments.
